message:
chore(beads): add bead for consumer Getting Started guide

bd-v857w: Consolidated getting-started.md for library consumers
(from open issue #10).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-30g1.6","title":"State Persistence E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of widget state persistence and restoration.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Save/Restore Cycle (persist_cycle_e2e.rs)
- Save on app exit
- Restore on app start
- Partial state handling
- Corrupted state recovery

### 2. Widget State (persist_widget_e2e.rs)
- ScrollView scroll position
- TreeView expanded nodes
- Table sort/filter state
- Tabs active index

### 3. Migration (persist_migrate_e2e.rs)
- Version upgrade handling
- Field addition migration
- Field removal migration
- Type change migration

### 4. Storage Backend (persist_storage_e2e.rs)
- File storage write/read
- Memory storage isolation
- Custom backend integration
- Concurrent access handling

### 5. Error Handling (persist_error_e2e.rs)
- File permission errors
- Disk full handling
- Partial write recovery
- Atomic save verification

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Save/restore operations with state size
- DEBUG: Migration steps executed
- TRACE: Field-level changes
- Metric: State file size over time

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/state_persistence/

## Crash Recovery Tests
- Kill during save
- Truncated file handling
- Checksum validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: State Persistence E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:03:10.054069518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:47:57.995543188Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T16:47:57.995515817Z","close_reason":"Implemented State Persistence E2E Test Suite with:

## Test Coverage
- 20 integration tests in crates/ftui-runtime/tests/state_persistence_e2e.rs
- Shell script for E2E testing in scripts/e2e/state_persistence/test_state_persistence.sh
- JSONL logging with run_id, seed, capabilities, timestamps, and checksums

## Test Scenarios Covered
1. Save/Restore Cycle: round-trip integrity, partial state, save-on-exit, restore-on-start
2. Widget State: ScrollView, TreeView, Table persistence
3. Migration: version upgrade handling, field addition, version isolation
4. Storage Backend: memory isolation, concurrent access, availability checks
5. Error Handling: corrupted entries, recovery, atomic saves
6. Property Tests: set/get identity, length invariant, dirty flag tracking

## Invariants Verified
- Round-trip integrity: State saved equals state restored
- Version isolation: Different versions don't corrupt each other
- Concurrent safety: Thread safety with 4-thread stress test
- Atomic writes: All entries saved together or none

## Note
Pre-existing compilation errors exist in:
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/widget_gallery.rs (LogWrapMode::Wrap, render_advanced_widgets)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs tests (present_ui signature mismatch)
These are unrelated to the E2E test work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30g1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-30g1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30g1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-30g1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":577,"issue_id":"bd-30g1.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FuchsiaMill claiming after completing bd-30g1.5. Will implement E2E test suite for state persistence covering:
- Save/restore cycle tests
- Widget state tests (ScrollView, TreeView, Table)
- Migration scenario tests
- Error handling tests","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:42:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30h","title":"Implement Sparkline and Chart widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: DATA VISUALIZATION

## Background
Sparkline and Chart widgets provide data visualization for metrics, trends, and timeseries. Common requirement for monitoring dashboards and CLI tools.

## Technical Requirements

### Sparkline Widget
- Compact single-line visualization
- Data as Vec<f64> or iterator
- Auto-scaling to min/max range
- Configurable bar character set (▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█)
- Color gradient based on value
- Reference line support (mean, threshold)

### Chart Widget (BarChart, LineChart)
- BarChart:
  - Horizontal/vertical orientation
  - Grouped and stacked modes
  - Labels and value display
  - Color per series
- LineChart:
  - Multiple series support
  - Point markers optional
  - Area fill under line
  - Axis labels and ticks
  - Legend positioning

## Design Rationale
Feature-gated for projects that need visualization. Sparkline is minimal overhead. Full charts use Canvas internally for rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sparkline with auto-scaling
- [ ] Sparkline color gradient
- [ ] BarChart with orientations
- [ ] BarChart grouped/stacked
- [ ] LineChart multi-series
- [ ] LineChart axis rendering
- [ ] Feature gate: charts

## Location: ftui-extras crate (feature = "charts")","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:19:54.798796411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:46:41.353849671Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:46:41.353827860Z","close_reason":"Implemented Sparkline (auto-scaling, block chars, color gradient), BarChart (vertical/horizontal, grouped/stacked, labels, custom colors), and LineChart (multi-series with Canvas/Braille rendering, axis lines, Y/X labels, legend). 29 tests. Feature-gated behind 'charts' which depends on 'canvas'.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30h","depends_on_id":"bd-3k4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-30t8a","title":"Diagram Type: Gantt","description":"Background
- Gantt charts are common in project planning and demos.

Scope
- Parse gantt syntax (sections, tasks, dates, dependencies, milestones).
- Respect dateFormat/axisFormat where supported; warn otherwise.
- Layout timeline with adaptive time scale to available width.
- Render bars with labels and progress markers.

Considerations
- Scale must be chosen deterministically based on width.
- Avoid label collision; allow ellipsis.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for scale selection and bar placement.
- Snapshot tests for multiple time ranges.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=gantt, size, tier, warnings.

Success Criteria
- Gantt charts render clearly at 80x24 and scale up gracefully.","notes":"Design plan (no code yet; files reserved by others):

Gantt implementation outline
1) AST: extend parse_gantt to handle `dateFormat` and `axisFormat` lines (new Statement variants, e.g., GanttDateFormat { format }, GanttAxisFormat { format }). Unsupported formats -> warning but still store raw string.
2) IR: add optional typed sub-IR to MermaidDiagramIr (docs/spec mentions typed sub-IRs). Proposed `gantt: Option<GanttIr>`, with title, sections, tasks, axis/date formats.
3) Meta parsing: parse GanttTask.meta CSV into structured fields: optional status (done/active/crit/milestone), optional id, optional start date (YYYY-MM-DD), optional duration (Nd / Nw), optional dependency `after <id>`. Any unrecognized token preserved in `raw_tags` with warning.
4) Timeline: compute task start/end deterministically. Anchored tasks with explicit start date use that; `after` tasks start at end of referenced id (warn if missing). If start date missing and no `after`, assign sequentially after prior task within section.
5) Scale selection: derive total day span; choose tick unit (day/week/month) based on available width. Deterministic thresholds (e.g., if days<=width- label_col, unit=day; else if weeks<=width-..., unit=week; else month).
6) Layout: label column width = min(max label len, area.width/3) with ellipsis. Row height 1 per task, blank row per section. Axis row on top, optional section headers.
7) Render: draw axis ticks + labels; draw task bars with border.horizontal; milestones as diamond glyph; done tasks use dim fg; critical tasks use bold/bright. ASCII fallback uses +-* etc via GlyphPolicy.
8) Tests: unit tests for meta parsing, dependency scheduling, scale selection; snapshot tests at 80x24 and 120x40 with fixture gantt_basic.mmd; JSONL logging fields: diagram_type=gantt, scale_unit, date_range, warnings.

Pending: wait for mermaid.rs / mermaid_layout.rs / mermaid_render.rs reservations to clear before editing.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleBadger","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:40:00.063666356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:45:01.146376390Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:40.008409256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-17w24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:51.529563760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:33.051178690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:36.785003577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:04.721951409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:04.436116583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-33fdz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:51:10.410838435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:27.338297492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","depends_on_id":"bd-u4fgt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:44:46.993531552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":970,"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DarkLynx claiming. LavenderForest appears inactive (not registered in Agent Mail). Starting Gantt diagram improvements: enhance IR normalization, improve layout/render, add test coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-06T23:51:30Z"},{"id":977,"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Gantt IR normalization and rendering complete (commit 83d929c0, pipeline update 76171ccd). IR stage: Done (GanttTitle/GanttSection/GanttTask handled in normalize_ast_to_ir). Render stage: Done (render_gantt with title, sections, task bars). Remaining: Gantt-specific layout (adaptive time scale), snapshots, PTY E2E, demo picker.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:19:01Z"},{"id":981,"issue_id":"bd-30t8a","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WildAnchor: Added 5 Gantt snapshot/E2E tests (commit 0bc39fb9): snapshot_gantt_basic_80x24, snapshot_gantt_basic_120x40, snapshot_gantt_stress_80x24, snapshot_gantt_stress_120x40, e2e_gantt_layout_structure. 4 blessed snapshot files. All 663 lib tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:45:01Z"}]}

- PTY E2E: mermaid harness test asserts at least one JSONL mermaid_render event with diagram_type=gantt, error_count=0, and no fallback fields.
- Parser: parse_gantt now accepts title:/section: and ignores known directive lines (dateFormat/axisFormat/etc.) so they don’t become tasks.
- Sequence: emit warnings for unknown note participants and for else/and without a matching alt/par; avoid misclassifying message-like control labels.
- Renderer/layout safety: saturating math in render_gantt + clamp negative label rect sizes; saturating_sub guards in ftui-layout shares and list/virtualized widgets.","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:05:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-315zw","title":"Mermaid Engine Config + Feature Flags","description":"Background
- Users need control over Mermaid rendering behavior and fallbacks.

Scope
- Define config struct + env vars for Mermaid engine:
  - enable/disable
  - glyph_mode (unicode/ascii)
  - tier_override (compact/normal/rich/auto)
  - max_nodes/max_edges
  - route_budget and layout_iteration_budget
  - max_label_chars/max_label_lines + wrap_mode
  - enable_styles, enable_init_directives, enable_links
  - link_mode (inline/footnote/off) + sanitize_mode (strict/lenient)
  - error_mode (panel/raw/both) and log_path
  - cache_enabled and capability_profile override
- Provide defaults and deterministic behavior.
- Document config in docs and surface in debug overlay.

Considerations
- Config changes must not cause nondeterministic output.
- Validate config with clear error messages.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for config parsing and validation.
- PTY E2E logs include config snapshot.

Success Criteria
- Config is stable and discoverable.
- Overrides behave as documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:58:30.147608584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T23:17:09.645955054Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T23:17:09.645936849Z","close_reason":"Config + envs implemented in crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs with validation/tests; docs/spec/mermaid-config.md documents fields/env; debug overlay shows MermaidConfig summary in crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-31go","title":"Cross-Platform Testing: Windows and macOS Validation","description":"## Background

README.md mentions Windows support is tracked in docs/WINDOWS.md and is still being validated. FrankenTUI should work consistently across platforms.

## Platforms to Test

1. **Linux** (primary) - Already well-tested
2. **macOS** - Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty
3. **Windows** - Windows Terminal, ConEmu, cmd.exe

## Test Matrix

| Platform | Terminal | Expected Support |
|----------|----------|------------------|
| Linux | kitty, alacritty, ghostty | Full |
| Linux | tmux, screen | Degraded (mux passthrough) |
| macOS | Terminal.app | Degraded (limited features) |
| macOS | iTerm2 | Full |
| macOS | Ghostty | Full |
| Windows | Windows Terminal | Full |
| Windows | ConEmu | Full |
| Windows | cmd.exe | Degraded |

## Tests Required

### Terminal Capabilities Detection
```rust
#[test]
#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
fn windows_terminal_detected() {
    std::env::set_var("WT_SESSION", "1");
    let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
    assert!(caps.true_color);
}

#[test]
#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
fn iterm2_detected() {
    std::env::set_var("TERM_PROGRAM", "iTerm.app");
    let caps = TerminalCapabilities::detect();
    assert!(caps.true_color);
}
```

### Cross-Platform E2E Script

Create `scripts/cross_platform_e2e.sh`:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run on CI with matrix strategy

echo "Platform: $(uname -s)"
echo "Terminal: $TERM"

# Build
cargo build -p ftui-demo-showcase --release

# Run headless tests
cargo test -p ftui-core -- --nocapture
cargo test -p ftui-render -- --nocapture

# Snapshot tests (should be identical across platforms)
cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test screen_snapshots
```

## CI Configuration

Add matrix builds to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
```yaml
cross-platform:
  strategy:
    matrix:
      os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
  runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
    - run: cargo test -p ftui-core
    - run: cargo test -p ftui-render
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Linux tests passing
- [ ] macOS tests passing
- [ ] Windows tests passing
- [ ] CI matrix configured
- [ ] Platform-specific capability detection tested
- [ ] Snapshot tests identical across platforms
- [ ] docs/WINDOWS.md updated with validated status

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-uo6v","title":"Create virtualization performance tests","description":"# Create virtualization performance tests

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Performance tests ensure virtualization achieves O(visible) not O(total) complexity.

## Performance Benchmarks

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod benchmarks {
    use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};
    
    fn bench_render_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("virtualized_list");
        
        // Generate 100K log lines
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        let list = VirtualizedList::new(virtualized);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24));
        let budget = RenderBudget::unlimited();
        
        group.bench_function("render_visible_only", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                list.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
                black_box(&buf);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_scroll_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("scroll");
        
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let mut virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_one_line", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.scroll(1);
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_page", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.page_down();
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_append_with_ring(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("log_ring");
        
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(10_000);
        
        group.bench_function("append_single", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                ring.push(LogLine::new("New log line"));
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("append_batch_100", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                for i in 0..100 {
                    ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Batch line {}", i)));
                }
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    criterion_group!(
        benches,
        bench_render_100k_items,
        bench_scroll_100k_items,
        bench_append_with_ring,
    );
    criterion_main!(benches);
}
```

## Performance Requirements

| Operation | Target | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Render 100K items | < 20ms | Only visible items rendered |
| Scroll 1 line | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Page scroll | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Append single | < 100ns | Amortized O(1) |
| Memory (100K items) | < 50MB | With 10K ring capacity |

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn render_time_scales_with_visible_not_total() {
    // 1K items
    let small = create_list(1_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    small.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let small_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // 100K items
    let large = create_list(100_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    large.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let large_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // Large should be similar to small (both only render ~24 items)
    assert!(large_time < small_time * 2);
}

#[test]
fn memory_bounded_by_ring_capacity() {
    let mut ring = LogRing::new(1_000);
    
    // Add 100K items
    for i in 0..100_000 {
        ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
    }
    
    // Only 1K in memory
    assert_eq!(ring.len(), 1_000);
    assert_eq!(ring.total_count(), 100_000);
}

#[test]
fn scrollbar_position_accurate() {
    let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..100).collect::<Vec<_>>());
    v.visible_count = 10;
    
    // At start
    v.scroll_offset = 0;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 0.0);
    
    // At end
    v.scroll_offset = 90;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 1.0);
    
    // At middle
    v.scroll_offset = 45;
    assert!((v.scrollbar_position() - 0.5).abs() < 0.1);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All benchmarks meet targets
- [ ] Render time O(visible)
- [ ] Memory bounded by capacity
- [ ] Scroll operations O(1)
- [ ] No allocations during scroll","notes":"## Property Tests

Add property tests for virtualization invariants:

```rust
use proptest::prelude::*;

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn scroll_offset_always_valid(
        total_items in 1usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_delta in -1000i32..1000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        
        v.scroll(scroll_delta);
        
        // Scroll offset must be valid
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset < total_items);
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset + v.visible_count <= total_items + v.visible_count);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn visible_range_always_within_bounds(
        total_items in 0usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_offset in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        v.scroll_offset = scroll_offset.min(total_items.saturating_sub(1));
        
        let (start, end) = v.visible_range(visible_count as u16);
        
        prop_assert!(start <= end);
        prop_assert!(end <= total_items);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn ring_never_exceeds_capacity(
        capacity in 1usize..10_000,
        insertions in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(capacity);
        
        for i in 0..insertions {
            ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
        }
        
        prop_assert!(ring.len() <= capacity);
    }
}
```

These property tests ensure virtualization invariants hold under all conditions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:43.392973094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:57:34.531721995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:57:34.531640772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uo6v","depends_on_id":"bd-20wz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-v6y","title":"Implement ASCII width fast-path optimization","description":"# Implement ASCII Width Fast-Path Optimization

## Background
The plan emphasizes that ASCII must dominate common workloads:
- Section 0.12: "ASCII fast-path must remain allocation-free and dominate common workloads"
- Section 4.3: ASCII width is a major optimization opportunity

Most harness/log output is ASCII. For ASCII:
- display width == byte length
- no grapheme segmentation needed
- no Unicode tables needed

## Scope
- Provide a fast, allocation-free `ascii_width` / `display_width` helper.
- Integrate it into text measurement / wrapping / truncation hot paths.

## Design
### Core API
```rust
/// Returns Some(width) if s is pure ASCII; otherwise None.
#[inline]
pub fn ascii_width(s: &str) -> Option<usize> {
    let bytes = s.as_bytes();
    if bytes.iter().all(|&b| b < 0x80) {
        Some(bytes.len())
    } else {
        None
    }
}

#[inline]
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize {
    ascii_width(s).unwrap_or_else(|| unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(s))
}
```

### Performance notes
- ASCII detection is O(n) but extremely cheap (tight loop over bytes).
- Optional micro-optimizations (still safe): use `memchr` or portable SIMD via `std::simd` if available.
- **Repo policy: unsafe is forbidden**, so any SIMD path must be safe or it is out of scope.

## Integration points
- Segment/Text measurement
- Wrapping/truncation (byte-fast for ASCII)
- Viewport/log viewer rendering

## Tests
- Pure ASCII returns correct width
- Mixed ASCII/Unicode returns None from `ascii_width`
- Empty string returns Some(0)
- Integration tests: wrapping/truncation correctness unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ascii_width` and `display_width` exist.
- [ ] ASCII path is allocation-free.
- [ ] Non-ASCII path matches `unicode_width`.
- [ ] Integrated into the ftui-text measurement code paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:52:10.389324927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790245126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790179993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-vins","title":"Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes","description":"## Background
The typewriter effect is limited to left-to-right reveal. A comprehensive reveal system enables dramatic text appearances for titles, announcements, and onboarding.

## Implementation

Add RevealMode enum (used as parameter, not nested in TextEffect):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum RevealMode {
    #[default]
    LeftToRight,    // Classic typewriter
    RightToLeft,    // Reverse typewriter  
    CenterOut,      // Expand from middle
    EdgesIn,        // Converge to center
    Random,         // Random order (deterministic with seed)
    ByWord,         // Word at a time
    ByLine,         // Line at a time (for multi-line)
}
```

Add TextEffect variant (consistent structure with others):
```rust
TextEffect::Reveal {
    mode: RevealMode,
    progress: f64,      // 0.0 = hidden, 1.0 = fully revealed
    seed: u64,          // For Random mode determinism
}

// Separate variant for gradient mask (different parameters)
TextEffect::RevealMask {
    angle: f64,         // Sweep angle in degrees (0 = left-to-right)
    progress: f64,      // Mask position 0.0-1.0
    softness: f64,      // Edge softness 0.0 (hard) to 1.0 (gradient)
}
```

### Visibility Calculation by Mode:

| Mode | Visible When |
|------|--------------|
| LeftToRight | char_idx < progress * total_chars |
| RightToLeft | char_idx >= (1-progress) * total_chars |
| CenterOut | distance_from_center < progress * max_distance |
| EdgesIn | distance_from_edge < progress * max_distance |
| Random | hash(seed, idx) < progress * total_chars |
| ByWord | word_idx < progress * total_words |
| ByLine | line_idx < progress * total_lines |
| RevealMask | rotated_position > threshold (with softness fade) |

### Usage:
```rust
// Simple left-to-right reveal
StyledText::new("Hello World")
    .effect(TextEffect::Reveal { 
        mode: RevealMode::LeftToRight, 
        progress: 0.5,
        seed: 0,
    })

// Dramatic gradient mask sweep
StyledText::new("WELCOME")
    .effect(TextEffect::RevealMask {
        angle: 45.0,
        progress: 0.5,
        softness: 0.3,
    })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reveal_ltr_first_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows first half
- test_reveal_rtl_last_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows last half
- test_reveal_center_out_middle_first: Center chars visible first
- test_reveal_edges_in_edges_first: Edge chars visible first
- test_reveal_random_deterministic: Same seed = same reveal order
- test_reveal_random_all_chars_reveal: progress=1 shows all
- test_reveal_by_word_whole_words: Partial progress shows complete words
- test_reveal_by_line_whole_lines: Lines reveal atomically
- test_reveal_mask_angle_0: Left-to-right sweep
- test_reveal_mask_angle_90: Top-to-bottom sweep  
- test_reveal_mask_softness_0: Hard edge (binary visible/hidden)
- test_reveal_mask_softness_1: Gradient fade across chars

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot CenterOut at progress 0.25, 0.5, 0.75
- Snapshot RevealMask at angle=45, softness=0.3

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
